summary: Solid second set with China>Rider and moving Stella Blue
blurb: The show shines in the second set, opening with a tight China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and flowing through an emotional Stella Blue before closing with a sing‑along Box of Rain. Jerry’s guitar is on point, Phil holds down a sturdy groove, and Vince provides solid keys despite occasional looseness.
review: June 9, 1994 at Cal Expo captured the Dead in a reliably tight form, especially in the second set. The night kicks off with a classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider that reviewers call the lift‑off for the evening, followed by an inspired stretch through Estimated Prophet, He's Gone, and a spacious Drums → Space segment that builds into a heartfelt rendition of The Last Time and a soul‑stirring Stella Blue. Jerry Garcia’s guitar work is described as “working tonight,” delivering moving phrasing on Stella Blue while slipping a lyric in Deal, and his vocal on the ballad is called heart‑wrenching. Phil Lesh anchors the groove and even slips in a brand‑new Lesh tune that adds fresh flavor. Vince Welnick’s keyboards sit solidly under the mix, though one listener felt he wasn’t as “on” as usual. The dual drummers, Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann, drive the band with strong, propulsive beats, highlighted during the space excursion. The set closes on a joyous Box of Rain encore that gets the crowd singing along. Overall, the performance is consistently tight with standout moments that make this show a worthwhile listen, especially via the excellent audience tape praised as the best 1994 audience recording available.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings highlight strong positives – China Cat > Rider, Stella Blue, Jack Straw and Box of Rain are repeatedly praised; only minor negatives (Deal lyric flub, Vince not fully on) appear. The overall tone is clearly positive, justifying a moderate sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience tape – crisp, sweet, very listenable with no major drop‑outs; praised as the best 1994 audience recording on the site.

key_highlights: China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider opens a strong second set.
Emotional Stella Blue performance with moving guitar phrasing.
Solid Jack Straw and sing‑along Box of Rain encore.
Powerful drums and space segment that drives the groove.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar was “working tonight,” delivering moving phrasing on Stella Blue and strong leads throughout, though he slipped a lyric in Deal.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh kept a solid bass foundation and even introduced a brand‑new Lesh tune that added fresh flavor to the set.
band_performance.Vince: Welnick’s keyboards were generally tasteful and supportive; one reviewer noted he wasn’t as “on” as usual but still provided solid fills.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming locked in with Billy, driving the groove and adding texture during the space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann matched Hart’s intensity, providing a powerful rhythmic backbone throughout the set.
